summary: Added US-845 desktop table integration coverage in src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts using the existing Vitest/jsdom integration seam. The new tests exercise representative desktop bidding, contract-family, contract play, all-pass, misere, deal-settlement, next-deal, and finished-result states through real game state plus renderer output, while reusing deterministic fixture style from the US-842 and US-844 dependency work. No production source was changed, and viewport-specific Playwright evidence was correctly left for downstream visual verification because the project exposes no Playwright runner entrypoint in package scripts.

tests_run.evidence: Red-green cycle completed. The first run failed on two incorrect desktop-state assumptions (AI-turn whist controls and misere declarer/trump fixture shape); after tightening the test fixtures to match the existing UI contract, the focused file passed with 6 tests.

tests_run.command: npm run test -- src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts src/ui/render.test.ts src/ui/controller.test.ts src/test/ruleFlow.integration.test.ts src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts
tests_run.evidence: 42 tests passed across the new US-845 integration file plus the affected renderer, controller, US-842 rule-flow, and US-844 scoring/result suites.
Vitest passed 14 files / 134 tests and the production build completed successfully with tsc and vite build.

verification_environment.verification_scope: US-845 desktop UI state integration coverage plus focused regression and full repo verify

node_package_scripts.verify: npm run test && npm run build

verification_environment.notes: Package scripts expose Vitest and the repo-wide verify/build path, but no formal Playwright runner entrypoint. Viewport-specific screenshot evidence therefore remains a downstream visual verification concern rather than an executable claim in this implementation task.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Desktop table shows current phase, active actor, legal human actions, contract, trump when applicable, current trick, player hands, score table, deal result, and final bullet result.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts; npm run test -- src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts; npm run verify
evidence_matrix.limitations: Evidence is deterministic in-process engine plus renderer integration, not browser viewport automation.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Desktop/laptop bidding, contract, whist, play, deal-result, and final-result states show the current phase, active actor, legal actions, and relevant score or contract context without blocking normal play.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts; src/ui/render.test.ts; src/test/ruleFlow.integration.test.ts; npm run test -- src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts src/ui/render.test.ts src/test/ruleFlow.integration.test.ts src/ui/controller.test.ts src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts
evidence_matrix.limitations: The acceptance evidence is state-contract integration coverage rather than a new desktop screenshot flow.

evidence_matrix.scenario: After a deal completes, the result remains understandable and the player can proceed to the next deal unless the bullet is complete.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts settlement/final scenarios; src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts; npm run test -- src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts; npm run verify
evidence_matrix.limitations: The continuation/final-stop behavior is validated through DOM integration and real state transitions, not through separate browser automation.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Representative desktop state families from the QA design are executable: bidding, contract-family, contract play, all-pass/misere, settlement, next-deal, and finished result.
evidence_matrix.limitations: The new file covers the minimum required state families rather than every named scenario variant from the design matrix.

evidence_matrix.scenario: US-845 integration tests validate the shipped desktop renderer/controller contract without modifying production UI code.
evidence_matrix.limitations: This task assumes the renderer/context behavior implemented in MZ-502 remains authoritative; no production UI changes were introduced here.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Desktop action exposure and phase progression remain bound to upstream legal-action and rule-flow behavior.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: docs/test-design/us-842-rule-phases.md; task-reports/PERSONAL-TASK-1142/attempt-1.json; task-reports/PERSONAL-TASK-1146/attempt-1.json; src/test/ruleFlow.integration.test.ts; npm run test -- src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts src/test/ruleFlow.integration.test.ts; npm run verify
evidence_matrix.limitations: This task intentionally does not re-prove the full rules engine; it relies on the green US-842 dependency suites and verifies the renderer projection stays aligned with them.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Desktop score table, deal-result, next-deal, and final-result surfaces remain aligned with the US-844 scoring/result behavior.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: task-reports/PERSONAL-TASK-1156/attempt-1.json; src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts; src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts; npm run test -- src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts; npm run verify
evidence_matrix.limitations: This task uses the accepted US-844 result panel semantics as a dependency rather than redefining score/result behavior.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts; npm run test -- src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts; npm run test -- src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts src/ui/render.test.ts src/ui/controller.test.ts src/test/ruleFlow.integration.test.ts src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: Coverage is representative desktop-state integration, not a full browser-run viewport automation matrix.

No Playwright runner entrypoint exists in project scripts, so this task classifies desktop/laptop viewport evidence as downstream visual verification work rather than claiming fresh screenshot automation.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: Task scope and prompt classification; src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts; src/ui/render.test.ts
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: This task keeps semantic desktop-state assertions executable, while dedicated accessibility verification remains owned by separate downstream work per task scope.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: npm run test -- src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts src/ui/render.test.ts src/ui/controller.test.ts src/test/ruleFlow.integration.test.ts src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts; npm run verify
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: Regression evidence is local deterministic suite coverage plus repo build verification.
Release evidence is scoped to the repo's existing test/build entrypoints in this worktree.
The task adds local integration tests only and touches no auth, backend, network, storage schema, or sensitive-data path.
